entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A legume crop for which the adequate plot size for trials was investigated.

entities.name: PLOT SIZE
entities.description: The dimensions of a test plot used in agricultural trials to ensure adequate data collection.

entities.name: REPLICATED YIELD TRIAL
entities.description: An experimental trial design used to measure crop yield across multiple replicates.

entities.name: HARVESTING INDIVIDUAL ROWS
entities.description: The process of harvesting specific rows from a plot to analyze the effect of plot size on yield estimates.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The agricultural productivity of the crop measured in the trial.

relationships.description: The study aims to determine the adequate plot size for pigeonpea trials.
The yield trial was conducted using pigeonpea.
Harvesting individual rows was the method used to investigate plot size adequacy.
The trial was designed to measure the yield of the crop.
Individual row harvesting was performed within the replicated yield trial.
